21.correct: Bharatanatyam, one of the oldest classical dance forms, originated in Tamil Nadu.
21.fact: Bharatanatyam was traditionally performed in temples by Devadasis and was revived in the 20th century.

22.correct: Chhath Puja is a four-day festival, involving rituals such as Nahay Khay, Kharna, Sandhya Arghya and Usha Arghya.

wrong.A: It lasts longer than two days.
wrong.B: It is not a three-day festival.
wrong.D: It is four days, not seven.

22.fact: The festival worships the Sun God and Chhathi Maiya, mainly in Bihar, Jharkhand and eastern UP.

23.correct: Ashim Kumar Ghosh was appointed Governor of Haryana in 2025.

wrong.A: Kavinder Gupta was appointed to another role/UT.
wrong.B: Manoj Kumar Singh is not the Haryana Governor.
wrong.C: Pusapati Ashok Gajapathi Raju was appointed Governor of another state.

23.fact: Governors are appointed by the President and hold office during the President's pleasure.

24.correct: Parag Jain was appointed the chief (Secretary) of the Research and Analysis Wing (R&AW) in 2025.

wrong.A: Ravi Sinha was the previous R&AW chief.
wrong.B: Praveen Sood heads the CBI, not R&AW.
wrong.C: Ajay Kumar is not the R&AW chief named here.

24.fact: R&AW is India's external intelligence agency, established in 1968.

25.correct: The amount of water (vapour) present in air is measured as humidity.

wrong.B: Density measures mass per unit volume, not water content of air.
wrong.C: Temperature measures heat, not water content.
wrong.D: Pressure measures force per unit area, not humidity.

25.fact: Relative humidity is the ratio of actual water vapour to the maximum the air can hold at that temperature.

26.correct: The Tehri Dam (on the Bhagirathi in Uttarakhand) is the highest dam in India.

wrong.A: Bhakra Nangal is high but lower than Tehri.
wrong.B: The Hirakud Dam is the longest, not the highest.
wrong.D: Sardar Sarovar is large but not the highest.

26.fact: The Tehri Dam is about 260.5 m high, one of the tallest dams in the world.

27.correct: Ziauddin Barani was a famous historian of the Delhi Sultanate, author of the 'Tarikh-i-Firoz Shahi'.

wrong.A: Amir Khusrau was a poet and musician of the Sultanate, not primarily a historian of this kind.
wrong.B: Abul Fazl was the historian of Akbar (Mughal), author of 'Akbarnama'.
wrong.C: Al-Biruni was a scholar who came with Mahmud of Ghazni, before the Sultanate.

27.fact: Barani's work is a key source for the reigns of the Tughlaq sultans.

28.correct: The AYUSH Aushadhi Gunavatta evam Utpadan Samvardhan Yojana (launched 2025) mainly aims to support AYUSH pharmacies/manufacturers in improving quality and production.

wrong.A: Promoting AYUSH dispensaries is not its core aim.
wrong.B: It does not regulate general food safety.
wrong.D: It is not limited to setting up urban AYUSH hospitals.

28.fact: AYUSH stands for Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha and Homeopathy.

29.correct: In September 2024 Maharashtra launched the 'Punyashlok Ahilyadevi Holkar Women Startup Yojana' to support early-stage women-led startups.

wrong.B: The Acharya Chanakya Kaushal Vikas Yojana is a skilling scheme.
wrong.C: 'Maharashtra Innovation Startup Initiative' is not the scheme named.
wrong.D: 'Maha-Fund Startup Assistance Scheme' is not the launched scheme.

29.fact: The scheme is named after the 18th-century Maratha queen Ahilyabai Holkar.

30.correct: Gir National Park (I) is in Gujarat; Bandipur National Park (II) is in Karnataka. So only I is in Gujarat.

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong; Bandipur is in Karnataka.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since Bandipur is not in Gujarat.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong because Gir is in Gujarat.

30.fact: Gir is the last natural home of the Asiatic lion.

31.correct: The 'geoid' means the true shape of the Earth - an Earth-like shape that is an irregular, slightly flattened sphere (oblate spheroid) rather than a perfect ball.

wrong.A: It does not mean a Sun-sized object.
wrong.B: It does not mean a planet without a moon.
wrong.D: It does not refer to a black hole.

31.fact: The geoid is defined by mean sea level extended under the continents - the reference surface for measuring elevations.

32.correct: The 'perigee' is the point on a satellite's orbit where its vertical distance from the Earth's surface is the least (closest approach).

wrong.A: The apogee is the farthest point from Earth.
wrong.C: 'Inclination' is the tilt of the orbit, not a distance point.
wrong.D: A Lagrange point is an equilibrium point in a two-body system, not the closest orbital point.

32.fact: For objects orbiting the Sun, the corresponding closest point is the 'perihelion'.

33.correct: The planned Indian Space Station (Bharatiya Antariksha Station) is expected to have 5 modules.
33.fact: ISRO aims to build the station in stages, with the first module targeted later this decade.

34.correct: In July 2025, the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bengaluru conducted the India study mapping indicators that drive ageing in the Indian population.

wrong.A: CDRI Lucknow was not the institute cited.
wrong.B: NIN Hyderabad was not the lead institute.
wrong.D: TIFR Mumbai was not credited with this study.

34.fact: Such studies help understand biological ageing markers specific to Indian populations.

35.correct: The United Nations Security Council has 10 non-permanent (temporary) members, elected for two-year terms.

wrong.A: 5 is the number of permanent members.
wrong.D: 15 is the total membership (5 permanent + 10 non-permanent).

35.fact: The five permanent members (P5) are the USA, UK, France, Russia and China, each with a veto.

36.correct: India joined the Badminton World Federation (then the International Badminton Federation) in 1936.

wrong.B: 1934 is when the IBF was founded by nine member nations, but India was not a founding member.
wrong.D: 1938 is not the year India joined.

36.fact: The IBF (now BWF) was founded in 1934; India became a member two years later, in 1936.

37.correct: As of mid-2025, the men's 200 m sprint world record stands at 19.19 seconds, set by Usain Bolt at the 2009 World Championships.

wrong.A: 18.19 seconds is faster than any recorded 200 m time.
wrong.C: 20.19 seconds is slower than the world record.
wrong.D: 21.19 seconds is far slower than the record.

37.fact: Usain Bolt also holds the 100 m world record of 9.58 seconds.

38.correct: Chemically inert gases like nitrogen and argon are filled in electric bulbs primarily to increase the life of the filament by preventing it from oxidising/evaporating quickly.

wrong.A: They are not added to make the bulb heavier.
wrong.B: They do not increase the brightness of the light.
wrong.D: They do not reduce production cost.

38.fact: Without an inert gas, the hot tungsten filament would burn out rapidly in oxygen.

39.correct: The 'Noot' is a solid percussion instrument shaped like an earthen pot, made of baked clay, found in Kashmir.

wrong.C: The 'Esraj' is a bowed string instrument.
wrong.D: The 'Pepa' is a horn/wind instrument of Assam.

39.fact: Pot-shaped clay instruments are played by striking the mouth and body for rhythm.

40.correct: Coke (a carbon-rich fuel from coal) is used in the manufacture of steel, where it acts as a fuel and a reducing agent in the blast furnace.

wrong.A: It is not used as a fuel for residential cooling.
wrong.B: It is not a food preservative.
wrong.C: It is not used to produce pharmaceuticals.

40.fact: In a blast furnace, coke reduces iron oxide ore to molten iron.
